# UX Data Performance API Security Checklist: Demand Threshold Alerts

**Purpose**: Validate the quality of requirements across UX, data, performance, API, and security concerns for UC-10 before implementation work begins.
**Created**: 2026-03-13
**Feature**: [spec.md](/root/311-forecast-system/specs/010-demand-threshold-alerts/spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are the operator-facing alert review needs defined beyond notification receipt, including what information must be visible for successful, partial, and failed deliveries? [Completeness, Spec §User Story 3, Plan §API Contract Direction]
- [x] CHK002 Are requirements defined for how forecast window types map to threshold evaluation scope across daily and weekly forecast products? [Completeness, Spec §FR-003, Plan §Technical Context]
- [x] CHK003 Are the required attributes of a threshold configuration fully specified, including active/inactive status, effective dates, and channel ownership assumptions? [Completeness, Spec §Key Entities, Plan §Data Model Direction]
- [x] CHK004 Are requirements defined for all API consumers that may trigger evaluations or read alert events, not just the operational manager persona? [Completeness, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §API Contract Direction]
- [x] CHK005 Does the specification define what performance expectations apply to non-success paths such as missing thresholds, suppressed duplicates, and retry/manual-review cases? [Gap, Spec §Success Criteria, Plan §Performance Goals]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK006 Is “prepare staffing and dispatch changes before service demand spikes” translated into observable UX or information requirements rather than left as an outcome-only statement? [Clarity, Spec §User Story 1]
- [x] CHK007 Is “relevant forecast value” unambiguous about which bucket, aggregation level, and time-window identity must be compared to a threshold? [Clarity, Spec §FR-003]
- [x] CHK008 Is “configured alert channel or channels” precise about whether the requirements govern supported channel types, ordering, and fallback semantics? [Clarity, Spec §FR-006, Spec §FR-007a]
- [x] CHK009 Is “without blocking later forecast publication workflows” defined with measurable limits or explicit non-blocking behavior criteria? [Ambiguity, Plan §Performance Goals]
- [x] CHK010 Is the distinction between `delivered` and `partial_delivery` requirement language clear enough to avoid conflicting interpretations during implementation and review? [Clarity, Plan §Data Model Direction, Plan §Implementation Notes]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK011 Are the duplicate-suppression requirements consistent between the user stories, edge cases, functional requirements, and plan data-model direction? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-013, Plan §Data Model Direction]
- [x] CHK012 Are delivery-success rules consistent between the spec’s “successfully delivered if at least one channel succeeds” wording and the plan’s separate `delivered` and `partial_delivery` statuses? [Consistency, Spec §FR-007a, Spec §FR-007b, Plan §Data Model Direction]
- [x] CHK013 Are the assumptions about existing threshold maintenance and notification-destination management consistent with the plan’s decision to exclude threshold authoring from this feature’s API surface? [Consistency, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §Phase 0 Research Decisions]
- [x] CHK014 Are category-only and category-plus-geography threshold requirements consistent with the plan’s precedence rule for more specific configurations? [Consistency, Spec §FR-002, Plan §Implementation Notes]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK015 Can the success criteria objectively verify UX value for operational managers, or do they measure only backend record creation and delivery timing? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §Success Criteria]
- [x] CHK016 Are performance targets measurable for every critical requirement path, including evaluation completion and alert retrieval, rather than only successful delivery latency? [Measurability, Spec §SC-002, Plan §Performance Goals]
- [x] CHK017 Can the requirement that “100% of missing-threshold and failed-delivery cases create traceable records” be objectively verified without a more explicit definition of required traceability fields? [Measurability, Spec §SC-004]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK018 Are primary UX scenarios defined for both passive receipt of alerts and later operational review of alert history and channel failures? [Coverage, Spec §User Stories, Plan §API Contract Direction]
- [x] CHK019 Are alternate data scenarios specified for cases where both category-level and geography-level thresholds could apply to the same forecast scope? [Coverage, Gap, Spec §FR-002, Plan §Implementation Notes]
- [x] CHK020 Are recovery requirements defined for what should happen after a delivery failure is retried or manually reviewed, or is that lifecycle intentionally out of scope? [Coverage, Recovery Flow, Spec §FR-010, Spec §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK021 Are API error and authorization scenarios covered by written requirements for invalid trigger requests, unauthorized access, and missing alert-event identifiers? [Coverage, Gap, Plan §API Contract Direction, Contract §paths]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK022 Are boundary-case requirements defined for threshold changes that occur between consecutive forecast evaluations for the same scope? [Edge Case, Gap, Spec §FR-011]
- [x] CHK023 Are data requirements defined for concurrent or overlapping forecast updates that evaluate the same threshold scope near-simultaneously? [Edge Case, Gap, Plan §ThresholdState concept]
- [x] CHK024 Are edge cases specified for an operational manager with zero usable channels after configuration drift, despite the assumption that at least one destination exists? [Edge Case, Assumption, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §ThresholdConfiguration]
- [x] CHK025 Are API pagination, result-size, or filtering boundaries defined for alert-event retrieval under larger operational histories? [Edge Case, Gap, Plan §API Contract Direction, Contract §GET /api/v1/forecast-alerts/events]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [x] CHK026 Are UX requirements defined for how partial delivery, suppressed duplicates, and configuration gaps should be communicated to operational users if surfaced in the product? [Non-Functional, UX, Gap, Spec §User Story 3]
- [x] CHK027 Are data retention and auditability requirements specified for evaluation runs, alert events, and channel-attempt records? [Non-Functional, Data, Gap, Spec §FR-012, Plan §Storage]
- [x] CHK028 Are performance requirements quantified for API read paths, not just for alert delivery timing? [Non-Functional, Performance, Gap, Plan §Performance Goals, Plan §API Contract Direction]
- [x] CHK029 Are security requirements specific about role boundaries between operators who receive alerts and administrators or schedulers who trigger evaluations? [Non-Functional, Security, Plan §Constitution Check, Plan §API Contract Direction]
- [x] CHK030 Are availability and operational safety requirements defined for how alert retrieval behaves when downstream notification providers are degraded but stored alert data remains available? [Non-Functional, Reliability, Gap, Plan §Constitution Check]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK031 Are upstream dependencies on published forecast lineage and existing threshold-management capability explicitly documented as requirement constraints rather than implicit implementation assumptions? [Dependencies, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §Phase 0 Research Decisions]
- [x] CHK032 Is the assumption that retry and manual-review workflows already exist validated by linked requirements, or should the spec state the minimum follow-up contract this feature depends on? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions, Spec §FR-010]
- [x] CHK033 Are external notification-provider dependency requirements defined for timeout handling, provider correlation identifiers, and failure classification vocabulary? [Dependency, Gap, Plan §Data Model Direction, Contract §NotificationChannelAttempt]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK034 Is there any conflict between the spec’s feature-level wording “alert record for each exceeded scope” and the plan’s introduction of a suppressed state where no new alert is created for a still-exceeded scope? [Conflict, Spec §SC-001, Spec §FR-013, Plan §Data Model Direction]
- [x] CHK035 Is “one alert event with multiple channel-attempt records” established clearly enough in the requirements, or does it currently appear only as a design choice in planning artifacts? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-007a, Plan §Phase 0 Research Decisions]
- [x] CHK036 Are the terms “forecast window,” “forecast window type,” and “forecast window key” used consistently enough across spec and plan to support one canonical data and API vocabulary? [Consistency, Spec §FR-003, Plan §Data Model Direction, Data Model §ThresholdState]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline.
- This checklist is intentionally lightweight and author-oriented, with equal coverage across UX, data, performance, API, and security requirement quality.
